document.title: Software Validation Report for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices, May 11, 2005
Requirements for Cybersecurity of Medical Devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software validation report for AXD software used with G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors, documenting requirements, design, verification, validation, traceability, hazards, cybersecurity, revision history, and unresolved anomalies.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical documentation by demonstrating software verification, validation, risk controls, traceability, cybersecurity controls, and lifecycle controls for the detector software.
keyPoints: AXD performs image acquisition, image correction, and pre-processing for the detector.
The software level of concern is concluded as Moderate.
Module verification and system validation tests are recorded as passed for common functions, image acquisition, calibration, firmware update, iMX8, FPGA app, cybersecurity, and error control modules.

Hazard analysis identifies risks including image transmission errors, x-ray exposure status, image correction errors, firmware errors, shock, parameters, Wi-Fi communication, sensor status, login, and data encryption, with residual acceptability marked acceptable after controls.
Unresolved anomalies are listed as None.

evidence.claim: The document is Software Validation Report A-TCF-10-131 Rev.2 dated 2026-04-14.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-TCF-10-131 Rev. No. 2 Software Validation Report Rev. Date 2026-04-14

evidence.claim: The report applies to the G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Validation Report for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The selectable detector models are G1417CW, G1717CW, G1417FW, G1717FW, G1417CWP, G1717CWP, G1417FWP and G1717FWP.
evidence.evidenceText: The Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector can be selected between G1417CW, G1717CW, G1417FW, G1717FW, G1417CWP, G1717CWP, G1417FWP and G1717FWP.
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Models

evidence.claim: AXD performs image acquisition, image correction, and pre-processing.
evidence.evidenceText: AXD is software of Detector that performs image acquisition, image correction, and pre-processing.

evidence.claim: The AXD software level of concern is Moderate.
evidence.evidenceText: According to below responds, we conclude a Moderate level of concern applies to the AXD software. Level of Concern: Moderate

evidence.claim: The level of concern section cites FDA software premarket guidance dated May 11, 2005.
evidence.evidenceText: The following section includes the information required in the FDA guidance document entitled “Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices”, May 11, 2005.

evidence.claim: The development process states it fulfills 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.
evidence.evidenceText: The development process fulfils the requirements 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity requirements include wireless ID/password access control and encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, and WPAPSK+TKIP.
evidence.evidenceText: Random access should be limited by setting ID and password when accessing the wireless network. Transmission data should be protected by applying encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, WPAPSK+TKIP, etc.

evidence.claim: Calibration includes offset correction, gain correction, and bad-pixel correction.
evidence.evidenceText: Calibration performs following compensations to acquired images. Offset Correction Gain Correction Bad-Pixel(Defect) Correction

evidence.claim: Software architecture consists of five independent modules, and acquisition modules correct and process raw detector images.
evidence.evidenceText: Software architecture consists of five independent modules. Acquisition modules provide the ability to correct and process raw images obtained from detectors.

evidence.claim: Module verification was evaluated as successfully installed software on a standard PC.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary: As a result of module verification, we positively evaluate the success in installed Software on standard PC.

evidence.claim: System testing found no unresolved bugs affecting safety and efficiency.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary: System testing verifies that all functions configured in each module are adequate. No unresolved bugs have been found in this software that affect safety and efficiency.

evidence.claim: The hazard analysis lists potential hazards during AXD use and evaluates frequency and severity.
evidence.evidenceText: The following chart shows a list of potential hazards that may occur during use of AXD. And analysis of what the potential cause would be along with the possible frequency and severity for each hazard has been indicated.

evidence.claim: Unresolved anomalies are listed as None.
evidence.evidenceText: Unresolved Anomalies No Problem Description Criticality Reason for Problem Potential Impact None

evidence.claim: Revision history states Rev.2 was issued April 14, 2026 due to software update.
evidence.evidenceText: 2 April 14, 2026 Revision due to software update

reviewFlags.reason: Authority metadata indicates MFDS/domestic, but the source text explicitly cites FDA guidance and 21 CFR 820.30; no MFDS authority text is present in the extracted source.

Conflicting revision metadata appears in the source: pages 1-35 show Rev. No. 2 and Rev. Date 2026-04-14, while pages 36-75 show File No A-TCF-10-131, Rev. No 0 with Rev. Date Nov/23/2024 or Nov/15/2024.
Some later test and traceability pages appear to preserve older headers, so test evidence confidence is medium where page headers conflict with the document cover revision.
